notes: Reproduced: SKFontManager.Default.MatchTypeface() returns null for all typefaces on macOS, including both custom fonts loaded via FromStream and system fonts loaded via FromFamilyName. The bug is broader than reported — it affects all loading methods, not just FromStream. MatchTypeface was marked [Obsolete] in 2.x because it did not work on all platforms, and was removed entirely in 3.x. The alternative API SKFontManager.Default.MatchFamily(familyName, style) works correctly. Tested on 2.88.3 (reporter's version), 2.88.9 (latest 2.88.x), and 3.119.2 (API removed). Cross-platform testing skipped since MatchTypeface was deprecated for not working on all platforms — the scope is likely universal.

reproductionSteps.description: Create a .NET 8 console project with SkiaSharp 2.88.3 (reporter's version).
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 2.88.3

reproductionSteps.description: Write repro code that loads a font via SKTypeface.FromStream() and calls SKFontManager.Default.MatchTypeface() with Normal, Bold, and Italic styles.

filesCreated.description: Console app that loads Arial Black from stream, calls MatchTypeface with different styles, prints results.

reproductionSteps.description: Build the repro project with SkiaSharp 2.88.3.
Run the repro. MatchTypeface returns null for all three font style queries (Normal, Bold, Italic), confirming the reported behavior.
reproductionSteps.output:
Loading font from: /System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Arial Black.ttf
Loaded typeface: FamilyName=Arial Black, Style=SkiaSharp.SKFontStyle
MatchTypeface(Normal): NULL
MatchTypeface(Bold): NULL
MatchTypeface(Italic): NULL
BUG CONFIRMED: MatchTypeface returned null for at least one style

reproductionSteps.description: Additional test: MatchTypeface also returns null for system fonts loaded via FromFamilyName, and for fonts loaded via FromFile. The issue is not specific to FromStream — MatchTypeface is broken for all font sources on macOS. However, MatchFamily works correctly as an alternative.
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet run (modified to compare FromFamilyName, FromStream, FromFile, and MatchFamily)
reproductionSteps.output:
=== MatchTypeface tests ===
System Arial: Arial
MatchTypeface(system, Bold): NULL
Stream ArialBlack: Arial Black
MatchTypeface(stream, Normal): NULL
File ArialBlack: Arial Black
MatchTypeface(file, Bold): NULL
=== MatchFamily tests (alternative API) ===
MatchFamily(Arial, Bold): Arial style=SkiaSharp.SKFontStyle
MatchFamily(Arial Black, Normal): Arial Black
Font manager family count: 180

reproductionSteps.description: Test with latest 2.88.x (2.88.9) in fresh project directory. Same result — MatchTypeface returns null.
reproductionSteps.output:
Loading font from: /System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Arial Black.ttf
Loaded typeface: FamilyName=Arial Black, Style=SkiaSharp.SKFontStyle
MatchTypeface(Normal): NULL
MatchTypeface(Bold): NULL
MatchTypeface(Italic): NULL
BUG CONFIRMED: MatchTypeface returned null for at least one style

reproductionSteps.description: Test with latest stable 3.119.2. MatchTypeface was removed in 3.x — build fails with CS1061.
reproductionSteps.output: error CS1061: 'SKFontManager' does not contain a definition for 'MatchTypeface' and no accessible extension method 'MatchTypeface' accepting a first argument of type 'SKFontManager' could be found

versionResults.notes: Reporter's version. MatchTypeface returns null for all typefaces (custom and system).
Latest 2.88.x. Same behavior — MatchTypeface returns null for all typefaces.
API removed in 3.x. Build fails with CS1061: SKFontManager does not contain MatchTypeface. The deprecated API was removed entirely.

errorMessages.primaryError: SKFontManager.Default.MatchTypeface() returns null for all typefaces on macOS, regardless of how the font was loaded (FromStream, FromFamilyName, FromFile)

actionability.reason: MatchTypeface was marked [Obsolete] in SkiaSharp 2.x because it did not work on all platforms, and was removed entirely in 3.x. The method returns null on macOS for all typefaces. The alternative API SKFontManager.Default.MatchFamily(familyName, style) works correctly. This is documented deprecated behavior, not a regression.

workarounds: Use SKFontManager.Default.MatchFamily(familyName, SKFontStyle.Bold) instead of MatchTypeface — this correctly finds system fonts by family name and style.
Load each font style variant (Regular, Bold, Italic) as separate font files using SKTypeface.FromStream() or SKTypeface.FromData().
Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x where MatchTypeface is removed, and use MatchFamily or direct font loading for each style.

actions.description: Explain deprecated API behavior and provide MatchFamily workaround
actions.comment: Reproduced on macOS with 2.88.3 and 2.88.9. MatchTypeface returns null for all fonts. Use SKFontManager.Default.MatchFamily(familyName, style) instead.

actions.description: Close as by-design — deprecated API removed in 3.x
